content:
# 1 Thessalonians Chapter 5 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus, PDF page 203 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/1TH_005_page_001.jpg

---

**5:1** Now concerning the times and the seasons, brothers, you-have no need [for anything] to-be-written to-you,

**5:2** for you yourselves know accurately that [the] day of-[the]-Lord as a-thief in [the] night so comes.

**5:3** When they-say, "Peace and security," then sudden destruction stands-upon them, just-as the birth-pain [comes upon] the-[woman]-having [child] in womb, and they-shall-not-at-all escape.

**5:4** But you, brothers, are not in darkness, that the day should-overtake you as a-thief;

**5:5** for all of-you are sons of-light and sons of-day. We-are not of-night nor of-darkness.

**5:6** So then let-us-not-sleep as the rest [do], but let-us-watch and let-us-be-sober.

**5:7** For the-[ones]-sleeping, by-night they-sleep, and the-[ones]-being-drunk, by-night they-are-drunk.

**5:8** But we, being of-day, let-us-be-sober, having-put-on [the] breastplate of-faith and of-love, and [as] a-helmet [the] hope of-salvation;

**5:9** because God did-not-appoint us unto wrath, but unto [the] obtaining of-salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ,

**5:10** the-[one]-having-died for us, so-that whether we-watch or whether we-sleep, together with him we-should-live.

**5:11** Therefore encourage one-another and build-up one the other, just-as also you-are-doing.

**5:12** Now we-ask you, brothers, to-know the-[ones]-laboring among you and presiding-over you in [the] Lord and admonishing you,

**5:13** and to-esteem them exceedingly-abundantly in love because-of their work. Be-at-peace among yourselves.

**5:14** Now we-exhort you, brothers: admonish the disorderly, encourage the faint-hearted, hold-fast-to the weak, be-long-suffering toward all.

**5:15** See that no-one renders evil for evil to-anyone, but always pursue the good, both toward one-another and toward all.

**5:16** Always rejoice.

**5:17** Unceasingly pray.

**5:18** In everything give-thanks; for this [is the] will of-God in Christ Jesus unto you.

**5:19** The Spirit do-not-quench.

**5:20** Prophecies do-not-despise.

**5:21** But test all-things; hold-fast the good.

**5:22** From every form of-evil abstain.

**5:23** Now the God of-peace himself sanctify you wholly, and [may] your whole [being] — the spirit and the soul and the body — be-kept blamelessly at the coming of-our Lord Jesus Christ.

**5:24** Faithful [is] the-[one]-calling you, who also will-do [it].

**5:25** Brothers, pray also concerning us.

**5:26** Greet the brothers all with a-holy kiss.

**5:27** I-adjure you [by] the Lord, [that] the letter be-read to-all the brothers.

**5:28** The grace of-our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **5:2 — "day of the Lord"**: Sinaiticus reads ΗΜΕΡΑ ΚΥΡΙΟΥ (hemera kyriou). The nomen sacrum ΚΥ is used for ΚΥΡΙΟΥ. This is the standard Sinaiticus reading, agreeing with the majority of witnesses.

2. **5:3 — "stands upon" (ephistatai)**: The Greek ΕΦΙCΤΑΤΑΙ is a present middle indicative of ΕΦΙCΤΗΜΙ, meaning "stands upon" or "comes upon suddenly." This vivid present tense conveys the unexpectedness and immediacy of the destruction. Some later manuscripts read the future ΕΠΙCΤΗCΕΤΑΙ.

3. **5:4 — "as a thief"**: Sinaiticus reads ΩC ΚΛΕΠΤΗC (hos kleptes), "as a thief" (nominative). Some manuscripts read ΩC ΚΛΕΠΤΑC (hos kleptas, "as thieves"), making the Thessalonians the subject of comparison rather than the day. The Sinaiticus reading is followed here.

4. **5:5 — "sons of light and sons of day"**: Sinaiticus reads ΥΙΟΙ ΦΩΤΟC ΕCΤΕ ΚΑΙ ΥΙΟΙ ΗΜΕΡΑC. The Hebraic idiom "sons of" (denoting character or belonging) is preserved literally.

5. **5:8 — "breastplate of faith and of love"**: Sinaiticus reads ΘΩΡΑΚΑ ΠΙCΤΕΩC ΚΑΙ ΑΓΑΠΗC. Paul adapts the imagery of Isaiah 59:17, substituting "faith and love" for Isaiah's "righteousness." The genitive construction is preserved.

6. **5:9 — "obtaining" (peripoiesin)**: ΠΕΡΙΠΟΙΗCΙΝ — this noun can mean "obtaining," "acquiring," or "possession." It denotes the active gaining of salvation, not merely its passive reception.

7. **5:13 — "exceedingly abundantly" (hyperekperissou)**: ΥΠΕΡΕΚΠΕΡΙCCΟΥ — a compound adverb unique to Paul (also 3:10), built from ΥΠΕΡ (beyond) + ΕΚ (out of) + ΠΕΡΙCCΟΥ (abundantly). The triple intensification is preserved in translation.

8. **5:14 — "disorderly" (ataktous)**: ΑΤΑΚΤΟΥC — a military term meaning "out of rank" or "undisciplined." In the Thessalonian context, this likely refers to those who had stopped working in expectation of the Lord's return (cf. 2 Thes 3:6-12).

9. **5:22 — "form" (eidous)**: ΕΙΔΟΥC — can mean "form," "kind," "species," or "appearance." Rendered "form" rather than "appearance" (which would suggest merely avoiding what looks evil). The sense is: abstain from every kind/variety of evil.

10. **5:23 — "wholly" (holoteleis)**: ΟΛΟΤΕΛΕΙC — a rare compound adjective (ΟΛΟC, "whole" + ΤΕΛΟC, "end/completion"), used only here in the NT. It means "complete in every part" or "through and through." The tripartite description "spirit and soul and body" that follows elaborates what "wholly" means.

11. **5:23 — Tripartite anthropology**: The sequence ΠΝΕΥΜΑ ΚΑΙ Η ΨΥΧΗ ΚΑΙ ΤΟ CΩΜΑ (spirit and the soul and the body) with the article before each noun emphasizes the completeness of sanctification across the entire person. This is not necessarily a technical statement of three-part human composition but an emphatic way of saying "your entire being."

12. **5:27 — "I adjure you"**: Sinaiticus reads ΕΝΟΡΚΙΖΩ ΥΜΑC ΤΟΝ ΚΥΡΙΟΝ. The verb ΕΝΟΡΚΙΖΩ ("I put under oath") is extremely strong — Paul shifts to the first person singular (from the plural used throughout the letter), indicating the gravity of this command. The accusative ΤΟΝ ΚΥΡΙΟΝ may function as an oath formula: "I adjure you by the Lord."

13. **5:25 — "also concerning us"**: Sinaiticus reads ΚΑΙ ΠΕΡΙ ΗΜΩΝ. Some manuscripts add "holy" (brothers), but Sinaiticus has the shorter reading here.

14. **5:28 — Closing benediction**: Sinaiticus reads Η ΧΑΡΙC ΤΟΥ ΚΥ ΗΜΩΝ ΙΥ ΧΥ ΜΕΘ ΥΜΩΝ — "The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you." Some later manuscripts add ΑΜΗΝ ("Amen") at the close. Sinaiticus omits it. The nomina sacra ΚΥ, ΙΥ, ΧΥ are expanded to their full forms.

15. **General note — column layout**: On this folio (page 203), 1 Thessalonians 5 begins in column 2 at verse 2 (continuing from 4:13-5:1 in column 1), continues through verse 11 into column 3, and verses 23-28 appear in column 4. The scriptio continua and nomina sacra conventions of Sinaiticus are maintained throughout.

— EMET
